report_name: Abyss Ransomware Hits German County Administration, Threatens to Leak Citizen Data

threat_actors.name: Abyss
threat_actors.description: A ransomware group that conducted a double-extortion attack against the Limburg-Weilburg County Administration, threatening to leak stolen data on its dark web leak site on June 1, 2026.

attack_patterns.name: Exfiltration Over C2 Channel
attack_patterns.description: The Abyss ransomware group exfiltrated sensitive data from the victim's network before encryption as part of its double-extortion playbook.

attack_patterns.name: Data Encrypted for Impact
attack_patterns.description: The Abyss ransomware was deployed to encrypt files across the Limburg-Weilburg County Administration's network to disrupt services and demand a ransom.

attack_patterns.name: Exploit Public-Facing Application
attack_patterns.description: Mentioned as a likely initial access vector used by Abyss and similar groups, targeting unpatched vulnerabilities in VPNs, firewalls, or other internet-facing government portals.

attack_patterns.name: Phishing
attack_patterns.description: Mentioned as a likely initial access vector, where targeted emails are sent to government employees to steal credentials.

attack_patterns.name: Valid Accounts
attack_patterns.description: Mentioned as a likely initial access vector, using credentials purchased from dark web markets.

attack_patterns.name: Inhibit System Recovery
attack_patterns.description: The threat actor likely deleted backups and shadow copies to prevent system recovery before deploying the ransomware payload.

attack_patterns.name: OS Credential Dumping
attack_patterns.description: Credential dumping from processes like lsass.exe is a common TTP for ransomware groups to escalate privileges and move laterally.

attack_patterns.name: Windows Management Instrumentation
attack_patterns.description: WMI was mentioned as a method for lateral movement used by ransomware groups.

malware.name: Abyss Ransomware
malware.description: A ransomware payload used by the Abyss group that performs double-extortion by exfiltrating data before encrypting it. It is known to append the '.abyss' extension to encrypted files.

tools.name: Cobalt Strike
tools.description: Mentioned as a tool used by Abyss and similar groups for command and control and lateral movement.

tools.name: rclone
tools.description: A legitimate data synchronization tool mentioned as being abused by ransomware groups to exfiltrate data.

tools.name: megacmd.exe
tools.description: A legitimate data synchronization tool mentioned as being abused by ransomware groups to exfiltrate data.

tools.name: PsExec
tools.description: Mentioned as a tool used for lateral movement in ransomware attacks.

tools.name: Windows Management Instrumentation
tools.description: Mentioned as a tool used for lateral movement in ransomware attacks.

identities.name: Limburg-Weilburg County Administration
identities.description: A German county administration in the state of Hesse targeted by the Abyss ransomware group, which claimed the attack on June 1, 2026 and threatened to leak citizen data.

infrastructure.name: Abyss Leak Site
infrastructure.description: A dark web site used by the Abyss ransomware group to publish claims of attacks and threaten to leak stolen data from victims.

indicators.name: Ransomware Precursor Reconnaissance Commands
indicators.pattern:
title: Ransomware Precursor Reconnaissance Commands
id: 2f8b1c9a-4e7d-4f8a-9b1c-9d8e7f6a5b4c
status: experimental
description: Detects the execution of common reconnaissance commands often used by ransomware attackers after initial access.
references:
  - https://dexpose.io/abyss-strikes-limburg-weilburg-county-administration/
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: 2026-06-01
tags:
  - attack.t1087.002
  - attack.discovery
level: medium
logsource:
  category: process_creation
  product: windows
detection:
  selection_whoami:
    Image|endswith: '\whoami.exe'
  selection_net:
    Image|endswith: '\net.exe'
    CommandLine|contains:
      - 'group "Domain Admins"'
      - 'user'
  condition: 1 of them
falsepositives:
  - Legitimate administrative activity.
indicators.description: Detects common reconnaissance commands like 'whoami' and 'net group' that are often run by attackers after gaining initial access to a network.

indicators.name: Abuse of Data Synchronization Tools for Exfiltration
indicators.pattern:
title: Abuse of Data Synchronization Tools for Exfiltration
id: 8a1b2c3d-4e5f-4a90-8bcd-ef1234567890
status: experimental
description: Detects the execution of legitimate data synchronization tools like rclone and megacmd, which are often abused by ransomware groups for data exfiltration.
references:
  - https://dexpose.io/abyss-strikes-limburg-weilburg-county-administration/
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: 2026-06-01
tags:
  - attack.t1041
  - attack.exfiltration
level: high
logsource:
  category: process_creation
  product: windows
detection:
  selection:
    Image|endswith:
      - '\rclone.exe'
      - '\megacmd.exe'
  condition: selection
falsepositives:
  - Legitimate use of these tools for data backup or synchronization.
indicators.description: Detects the use of legitimate data synchronization tools like rclone.exe and megacmd.exe, which are frequently abused by ransomware actors for data exfiltration before encryption.

indicators.name: Abyss Ransomware File Creation
indicators.pattern:
title: Abyss Ransomware File Creation
id: 5d6e7f8a-9b1c-4d8e-7f6a-5b4c3d2e1f0a
status: experimental
description: Detects the creation of files with the '.abyss' extension, which is indicative of the Abyss ransomware encrypting files.
references:
  - https://dexpose.io/abyss-strikes-limburg-weilburg-county-administration/
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: 2026-06-01
tags:
  - attack.t1486
  - attack.impact
level: critical
logsource:
  category: file_event
  product: windows
detection:
  selection:
    TargetFilename|endswith: '.abyss'
  condition: selection
falsepositives:
  - Unlikely.
indicators.description: Detects the creation of files with the '.abyss' extension, a strong indicator of an active Abyss ransomware infection.

indicators.name: Exfiltration Tool Execution
indicators.x_netsecio_sigma_rule:
title: Exfiltration Tool Execution
id: 1b2c3d4e-5f6a-4b91-8cde-f12345678901
status: experimental
description: Detects the execution of legitimate data synchronization tools like rclone and megacmd, which are often abused by ransomware groups for data exfiltration.
references:
  - https://dexpose.io/abyss-strikes-limburg-weilburg-county-administration/
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: 2026-06-01
tags:
  - attack.t1041
  - attack.exfiltration
level: high
logsource:
  category: process_creation
  product: windows
detection:
  selection:
    Image|endswith:
      - '\rclone.exe'
      - '\megacmd.exe'
  condition: selection
falsepositives:
  - Legitimate use of these tools for data backup or synchronization.
indicators.description: Detects the execution of common data exfiltration tools rclone.exe and megacmd.exe.

indicators.name: PsExec Tool Execution
indicators.x_netsecio_sigma_rule:
title: PsExec Tool Execution
id: 2c3d4e5f-6a7b-4c92-8def-123456789012
status: experimental
description: Detects the execution of PsExec, a tool commonly used by attackers for lateral movement.
references:
  - https://dexpose.io/abyss-strikes-limburg-weilburg-county-administration/
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: 2026-06-01
tags:
  - attack.t1570
  - attack.lateral_movement
level: medium
logsource:
  category: process_creation
  product: windows
detection:
  selection:
    OriginalFileName: 'psexec.c'
    Description: 'Execute processes remotely'
  filter:
    Image|endswith: '\PSEXESVC.exe'
  condition: selection and not filter
falsepositives:
  - Legitimate administrative use of PsExec.
indicators.description: Detects the execution of the PsExec tool, which is often used for lateral movement.

relationships.source_name: Abuse of Data Synchronization Tools for Exfiltration
